scope: TG-P14 AMD recovery and pure attention landing
supersedes: Prior hand-authored terminal artifact (2026-07-01T22:36) that preserved PRE-pin failing shared-weight data plus an AMD-wedge blocker. Host was power-cycled (boot 22:42); this is the fresh post-reboot verified result.
script_top_verdict_note: The script's PASS verdict (TG_P10_1_PASS_REG_REPRO_PINNED) requires fails!=[] AND devec_nan=True by design -- it is a fix-OFF 'is the bug still present' pin. Run with REDUCE_ACC_UPCAST_FIX=1 the failure modes are resolved (fails=[], devec_nan=False), so the script downgrades to NOT_MINIMAL and exits 1. TG-P14.3 grades on per-case compile+numeric correctness, which is the intended success shape.
determinism: Two consecutive fix-on runs produced identical results (fails=[], devec_nan=False). Repro is generated-UOp-only, no model weights.

case_results.scratch_note: This case exercises the previously-unverified opts_to_apply=() pin on flash_fused_gmax_combine_kernel. It was the case the handoff flagged as numerically mismatching (max_abs_err ~2.44, odd output slots left zero). It is now numerically correct, verifying both the devectorizer distinct-slot pass (compile) and the opts_to_apply=() pin (numeric).
